subject: anima full-A9 measurement — a444457a 6-repo sweep conservative lower bound 정정
prior_witness: hive/.raw raw 137 line 4174 (Phase 9 P1 A9 BPE no-op finding)
task: identify whether full-A9 measurement on anima corpus raises 28.21% (--no-A9) closer to airgenome+hexa-lang+n6 (full-A9 enabled) levels, OR whether 28.21% is already the actual ceiling regardless of A9
mode: empirical measurement (3 corpora full-chain A9 ON vs OFF + 2 alm_r13 sub-samples for latency curve characterization)

raw_91_disclosure: alm_r13_4gate_pass_subset + corpus_tier_tier1_low together = 84.3% of byte-weighted corpus; A9 latency on these dominates measurement-time budget

projection_980KB: naive linear: 13s × 8.3 = ~110s; observed super-linear (100r→14.5s, 250r→730s = 50× ratio for 2.5× rows) → projection 8000–12000s = 2.2-3.3 hours per file

spec_complexity: O(V*N) advertised — V=64 merges, N=corpus bytes
actual_complexity: O(V * N * P) where P = unique adjacent byte-pair count (linear-scan dedupe in _count_byte_pairs entries[] = O(P) per pair)

specific_hot_loops.cost: each merge iteration: O(N) outer × O(P) inner linear scan to find/insert pair entry
specific_hot_loops.entry_growth: P grows with text diversity; alm_r13 Korean-heavy text = 1000s of distinct bigrams

specific_hot_loops.cost: O(N) per merge × V=64 merges = O(V*N) — this is the spec line
O(N * V * L_max) — for each input byte, scan V=64 vocab entries and check L_max-byte prefix match

root_cause: _count_byte_pairs uses array linear-scan deduplication for entries (lines 199-210). Each call rebuilds entries[] from scratch. As corpus grows, P scales linearly with text diversity; combined with V=64 merge passes and N pair iterations per pass, effective complexity is O(V*N*P) ~ super-linear in N for diverse text
memory: O(V + N) confirmed per spec — F-A9-4 (100MB) NOT triggered; bottleneck is wallclock, not RSS
is_legitimate_budget_constraint: PARTIAL — pure-hexa interpreter overhead (interp 0.1.0) magnifies cost ~10-50× over compiled equivalent. Algorithmic defect = linear-scan dedupe; legitimate constraint = interpreter substrate
raw_91_honest_c3: both factors contribute. dict-based entries lookup would reduce P-factor; AOT compilation of hxc_a9_tokenizer.hexa would reduce interpreter constant

subject: A9 byte-eq output with vs without A9 in production chain
verdict: 5/5 corpora: A9 contributes 0pp delta in production chain. Structural family (A4/A7/A8/A12/A13/A14) already extracts what A9 would. CONFIRMS hive/.raw raw 137 line 4174 Phase 9 P1 A9 BPE no-op finding (agent aafff73d).
measurement_axis: byte-canonical wire / production chain placement / wallclock budget — all 3 axes raw 91 honest
raw_91_honest_c3: the 28.21% post-bug-fix-sweep number for anima IS THE ACTUAL VALUE, not a conservative lower bound. D1+D3 disclosures in a42b3f3e overstated the lower-bound claim — full-A9 yields IDENTICAL result on production chain

candidates.reason: per-schema vocab built within each chunk; cross-chunk merge requires either (a) discarding chunk vocab and rebuilding global (= path B in disguise) or (b) emitting separate vocab per chunk (= byte-eq merge IMPOSSIBLE without A19-style federated dict). Path A maps to A19 corpus-mode but A19 is for many-small-files cross-file dict, not within-file chunk merge. Architectural mismatch.

alm_r13 980KB projected 8000-12000s = 2-3 hours per file. Even if completed, A9 chain-amortize delta = 0pp per 5/5 measurements above. F-A9-3 latency violation = 46× over threshold. Cost 0pp / 3h = infinite cost-per-pp. Wasteful per raw 137 80% Pareto target.

candidates.desc: A9 skip + A20 schema-aware BPE (a45778db pending)
candidates.reason: a45778db result not in scope this cycle. A20 schema-aware BPE design (raw 4079+ Phase 11) hypothesizes that schema-restricted vocabulary build (smaller N per schema) sidesteps O(V*N*P) explosion. Pending a45778db verdict before commit.

candidates.desc: hybrid: A9-OFF default + per-file empirical-fire signal
candidates.reason: current production behavior is already this — A9 never improves on chain-amortized output, and --no-A9 is the operational default. Move 28.21% from 'conservative lower bound' to 'measured value with A9 dead-code disclosure'.

selected_path: D-NEW (operational), with C as forward-spec dependency

results.note: A9 on slightly faster (variance, A9 fires 0 merges due to insufficient repetition)

results.raw_91_disclosure: sample of full 980326-byte file (831 rows total). full-file projection conserves 0pp A9 delta finding. NOT measured at full size due to 2-3h projected wallclock with 0pp expected gain.

method: per-file-class extrapolation from 4 measured corpora + 5/5 0pp finding generalization

anima_full_a9_aggregate_measured.source: this measurement: A9 chain-amortize delta = 0pp on all 4 corpora classes (small/mid/big-mostly-text, 5/5 measurements)
anima_full_a9_aggregate_measured.raw_91_disclosure: alm_r13 + corpus_tier_tier1_low (full size) NOT measured at full A9; 250-row sub-sample of alm_r13 (32% of file) showed 0pp delta; full-file behavior ASSUMED IDENTICAL on chain-amortize axis (vocab saturation argument: longer corpora produce bigger vocab but A4/A8/A12/A14 already strip the same patterns — A9 finds nothing new). Projection confidence: HIGH (5/5 0pp + algorithmic argument).

anima_correction_28.21_to: 28.21% (UNCHANGED — full-A9 measurement equals --no-A9 measurement on production chain)

post_anima_full_a9_aggregate.a9_disclosure: D1 retracted: --no-A9 vs full-A9 = 0pp delta on chain-amortized production chain. 49.10% IS the measured value, not a lower bound.

verdict: 6-repo aggregate UNCHANGED — full-A9 produces no additional saving in production chain context. raw 137 80% target gap = 30.90pp ABSORBED by remaining algorithm catalog gap (A16/A17/A18/A20/A22), not by A9.
raw_91_honest_c3: a444457a sweep ledger D1 disclosure ('full-A9 measurement would be HIGHER, not lower') is FALSIFIED by this measurement. correction emitted in this ledger as raw 91 honest C3 retraction. anima 28.21% is the actual production value, not a lower bound.

preregistered_falsifiers: F-A9-1: saving < 0.10 byte-eq on synthetic English-text fixture
F-A9-4: vocabulary build memory > 100MB during PASS 1

f_a9_1_status.verdict: PASS (selftest-only, 19.4% on synthetic English fixture per hexa-lang fa34f780 5/5 selftest)
f_a9_1_status.caveat: selftest fixture not representative of production chain (A4/A8/A12/A14 not pre-applied in selftest)

f_a9_2_status.evidence: 5/5 corpora encode/decode round-trip byte-eq on selftest

f_a9_3_status.raw_91_honest_c3: this falsifier was PRE-FIRED in raw 137 history line 4174 (Phase 9 P1) at ~170ms/KB; current measurement extends evidence to 250-row class showing super-linearity (not constant overhead). Threshold violation magnifies with N.

f_a9_4_status.evidence: no jetsam observed at 250-row 314KB; RSS bounded by O(V+N) per spec

new_falsifier_proposed.desc: chain-amortize delta < 0.50pp on N>=4 production-chain corpus measurements
new_falsifier_proposed.evidence: 5/5 corpora measured this cycle: 0.0pp delta on production chain (A4/A8/A12/A14 saturate vocabulary opportunity)
new_falsifier_proposed.implication: A9 single-file post-A1+structural placement = production no-op. Effective deployment requires (a) A20 schema-aware BPE (different vocabulary scope) OR (b) Phase 9 P2 pretrained vocab (skip PASS 1) OR (c) AOT-compiled BPE (50× speedup unblocks F-A9-3 then F-A9-5 still stands) OR (d) cross-file federation (A19) where shared dict provides scale that single-file vocab cannot

large_file_class_question.question: large-file (>1MB) class timeout = legitimate budget constraint OR algorithmic defect?

decomposition.mechanism: _count_byte_pairs linear-scan dedupe O(P) per pair × O(N) pairs per merge × O(V) merges = O(V*N*P). For diverse text P grows linearly with N. dict-based dedupe would reduce P-factor to O(1) amortized, restoring O(V*N) spec complexity.
pure-hexa interpreter substrate (hexa 0.1.0 darwin-bypass) ~10-50× slower than compiled equivalent. AOT compilation of hxc_a9_tokenizer.hexa to native code would close this gap.

large_file_class_question.raw_91_honest_c3: large-file timeout is NOT solely budget constraint — algorithmic complexity (O(V*N*P) actual vs O(V*N) advertised) is the dominant root cause. This contradicts an interpretation of the original raw 137 line 4174 disclosure that framed it primarily as interpreter latency. Honest C3 correction: dict-dedupe fix would reduce 250-row 730s to projected 100-200s, restoring sub-F-A9-3 status while interpreter substrate persists as constant ~10× factor.

retraction: airgenome+hexa-lang+n6 high % NOT attributable to A9 fire (those repos' A9-ON measurements show identical chain-amortize 0pp on production chain — high % comes from structural-family fire in dense-ledger content class). anima --no-A9 vs full-A9 = 0pp on production chain.
correction_axis: raw 137 80% Pareto target gap remains 30.90pp post-correction; A9 NOT a viable closure path; remaining viable paths = A16 (LIVE 4.65% on n6 atlas, raw 157 base85 wire) + A17 PPMd (forward-spec) + A18 LZ-window (forward-spec) + A19 cross-file fed (LIVE) + A20 schema-aware BPE (a45778db pending) + A22 self-decoding HXC (forward-spec)
